channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Draft budget plan proposes deep cuts across federal health programs
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A draft budget proposal circulating among federal officials would dramatically deepen cuts at the nation's top health agency, eliminating some public health programs entirely and serving as a roadmap for more mass firings. The document suggests a cut in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MIKE STOBBE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Gabbard declassifies Biden counterterrorism strategy, confirms push for information-sharing with Big Tech
item.description: A newly declassified Biden-era strategy to counter domestic terrorism called for information sharing with Big Tech, banning assault weapons and curbing ghost guns.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ethics investigation of state Senate president prompts conflict-of-interest rules scrutiny
item.description: Attorney and former State DFL House Majority Leader Ryan Winkler joined MPR News host Cathy Wurzer on Morning Edition on Thursday to talk about conflicts of interest at the Legislature.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump policies top the talking points at Minnesota lawmaker town halls
item.description: Lawmakers from both parties fielded questions and comments from constituents in person. President Donald Trump’s policies on immigration, health care and budget cuts generated the most heat.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, Dan Kraker, and Hannah Yang, MPR News

item.title: Republicans consider increasing taxes on the rich in break from party orthodoxy
item.description: Republicans in Congress are considering increasing taxes on the rich as a part of President Trump’s “big, beautiful bill” of ambitious legislative priorities, a striking development that breaks with decades of party orthodoxy and is spurring alarm bells from traditional conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Trump says trade deals could be done within a month
item.description: After saying earlier in the day that he was “in no rush” to finish trade deals, President Donald Trump said he thought he could wrap up tariff talks “over the next three or four weeks.”.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Clashing Over Campus Control: Lawmakers vs. DeSantis on transparency
item.description: For years, lawmakers have speculated that the leaders who run the state's Universities and Colleges have been gifted these positions based on their political loyalties to the Governor.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump joins tariff talks with Japan as US seeks deals amid trade wars
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Wednesday inserted himself directly into trade talks with Japanese officials, a sign of the high stakes for the United States after its tariffs rattled the economy and caused the administration to assure the public that it would quickly reach deals.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, MARI YAMAGUCHI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge says labor unions' lawsuit over DOGE access to Labor Department systems can move forward
item.description: A federal judge says he won't dismiss a lawsuit from labor unions seeking to block Elon Musk's team from accessing systems at the Labor Department.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: GOP lawmakers investigate nonprofit founded by Casey DeSantis; executive director resigns
item.description: The program has been under scrutiny by Republicans in the Florida House, at the same time as the first lady considers a run for governor.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: IRS asked to revoke Harvard's tax-exempt status
item.description: Only the IRS can review and revoke the existing status of taxpaying entities and has traditionally done so without presidential interference, as mandated by tax laws.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Newsom says California will sue over Trump tariffs
item.description: “President Trump’s unlawful tariffs are wreaking chaos on California families, businesses, and our economy — driving up prices and threatening jobs," Newsom said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: What happens next after judge warns of possible contempt prosecution over deportation flights order
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge's ruling that the Trump administration appears to have willfully violated his order to turn around planes of migrants headed for El Salvador increases the prospect of officials being held in criminal contempt of court and potentially facing possible prosecution. U.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, MARK SHERMAN and MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wisconsin squeaks past Minnesota for the top spot in voter turnout
item.description: The Minnesota Secretary of State confirms Wisconsin narrowly surpassed Minnesota with voter turnout in the 2024 election.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters and Peter Cox, MPR News

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's wife sought restraining order in 2021, DHS says
item.description: The wife of Kilmar Abrego Garcia filed for an order of protection against her husband in 2021, saying he was violent toward her.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: RFK Jr. decries ‘epidemic denial’ amid increasing autism rates
item.description: The CDC has found a record 1 in 31 children are diagnosed with autism by the age of 8 in the United States.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, NewsNation

item.title: Hegseth adviser put on leave amid leaks probe
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s adviser was escorted out of the Pentagon on Tuesday and put on administrative leave amid a probe into information leaks at the department.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's lawyer says no proof of MS-13 affiliation
item.description: Kilmar Abrego Garcia's attorney, Benjamin Osorio, joined "CUOMO" for a live exclusive interview to discuss his client's status.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Ten Commandments in classrooms 'grave concern' to Baptist pastor
item.description: A bill requiring the display of the Ten Commandments in public buildings and classrooms was signed into law in Arkansas on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: In their words: What judges and Trump's government say about Abrego Garcia's mistaken deportation
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration has dug in on its contention that his government should not have to repatriate Kilmar Abrego Garcia, despite court rulings that he was wrongly deported and should be returned to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Medicaid puzzle confronts Minnesota lawmakers. Federal cuts could hit health safety net program
item.description: Republicans in Washington are working on slashing the federal budget to help pay for a slate of tax breaks. State lawmakers know it could mean a health care funding hole, but they don’t know how much they’ll have to backfill.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Trump assassination plot part of domestic terrorism rise: Ex-FBI
item.description: Ex-FBI special agent Stuart Kaplan joined "NewsNation Now" to discuss how it is increasing across the United States, largely thanks to social media.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: White House doctor says Trump is 'fully fit' in medical report
item.description: President Trump lost 20 pounds, lowered “bad” cholesterol and has “diverticulosis and a benign polyp,” according to a medical report released by the White House.
item.dc:creator: Luke Garrett, MPR News

item.title: Trump admin tells judge man wrongly deported to El Salvador is alive, still detained
item.description: On Friday, federal judge Paula Xinis had ordered the Trump administration to provide daily updates to return Kilmar Armando Abrego Garcia to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster, MPR News

item.title: Trump announces $600 million in new deals with five law firms
item.description: The deals bring to nine the number of firms that have struck agreements with Trump in the wake of numerous actions targeting major law offices.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5
